title: Toba

'''Toba''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] who [[Pilot/Legends|flew]] the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] near the [[Naboo system/Legends|Naboo system]] as a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] during the [[Great Peace of the Republic]]. An adventurous spirit from an early age, Toba was born to influential [[Parent/Legends|parents]] in the [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]]—his mother the former [[Rep/Legends|Rep]] [[Neesada Bari]], and his father, the powerful [[Tribubble bongo/Legends|bongo]] manufacturer [[Bullba Bari|Bullba]]. The Gungan enjoyed his youth, embarking on dangerous excursions of his own design and living as a playboy until the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]], during which he entered service in the [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|Gungan Grand Army]] and fought in the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of the Great Grass Plains]]. Inspired by the combat prowess and offworld exploits of his [[Bombad General/Legends|Bombad General]], [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]], Toba aspired to a life away from Otoh Gunga and [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]].

After the Invasion of Naboo, Toba was among the first colonists on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Ohma-D'un/Legends|Ohma-D'un]], and soon found work as a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] pilot, ferrying goods and individuals between the moon and Naboo. The Gungan ultimately progressed into two years of service with the [[Naboo Royal Space Fighter Corps/Legends|Naboo Royal Space Fighter Corps]], as a part of the anti-[[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] patrol [[Iron Cesta Flight]]. Toba found it difficult to adapt to the regimentation of military life however, and withdrew from service, pooling his resources to purchase the [[B'zabuu-class transport|''B'zabuu''-class transport]] ''[[Sea Killer]]'', and begin life as a smuggler. In spite of his new exploits along the hyperlanes, Toba maintained Ohma-D'un as his base of operations.

==Biography==
===Idols and playthings===
Toba was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Gungan/Legends|Gungan]] born into a wealthy family in the city of [[Otoh Gunga/Legends|Otoh Gunga]] on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] during the [[Great Peace of the Republic]]. His [[Parent/Legends|father]], [[Bullba Bari|Bullba]], was the owner of [[BullbaBong]], a successful [[Tribubble bongo/Legends|bongo]] manufacturer and reseller—and his mother was [[Neesada Bari]], who had served on the [[Gungan High Council/Legends|Gungan High Council]] as a [[Rep/Legends|Rep]] three times. Toba had siblings, but of Bullba and Neesada's children he was the most adventurous and inquisitive of the lot, frequently exploring [[Cave/Legends|caves]] and taunting [[Gooberfish/Legends|gooberfish]]. While still a [[Youngling/Legends|child]], Toba rode on the back of a deadly [[Opee sea killer/Legends|opee sea killer]], and survived. Nevertheless, the Gungan was content to enjoy his family's wealth, and lived as a playboy for several years.<ref name="tsas" />

In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], Naboo was [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invaded]] by forces of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]], a corporate entity protesting the taxation of trade routes.<ref name="tpm">{{Film|I}}</ref> Although Otoh Gunga faced [[Siege of Otoh Gunga|reprisals]] from Federation forces commanded by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] and [[OOM command battle droid/Legends|Commander]] [[OOM-9/Legends|OOM-9]],<ref name="gb">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]''</ref> Toba survived to join the [[Gungan Grand Army/Legends|Gungan Grand Army]], against the wishes of his mother. The Gungan was one of the youngest to serve in the Grand Army as it assembled in preparation for the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of the Great Grass Plains]],<ref name="tsas" /><ref name="tpm" /> in which a force led by [[Bombad General/Legends|Bombad Generals]] [[Tobler Ceel/Legends|Tobler Ceel]] and [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]] would engage OOM-9's [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|army]]. Meanwhile, the newly-allied forces of the [[Royal House of Naboo/Legends|Royal House of Naboo]] and [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] would reclaim the planet's capital [[City/Legends|city]], [[Theed/Legends|Theed]], and the [[Naboo Royal Space Fighter Corps/Legends|Royal Space Fighter Corps]] would attempt to destroy the Federation's [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class Droid Control Ship]] [[Vuutun Palaa/Legends|''Vuutun Palaa'']], which in turn would deactivated OOM-9's forces.<ref name="tpmn">[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' novelization]]</ref>

Toba was assigned to Binks's command, as as the Battle of Grassy Plains took place, the young Gungan discovered that he had a passion for the art of combat. He particularly marveled at his Bombad General's battle prowess,<ref name="tsas" /> as Binks managed to dispatch a number of [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-Series battle droids]], a [[Droideka/Legends|Droideka]], and even an [[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|Armored Assault Tank]] largely by his own hand.<ref name="tpm" /> It was common knowledge among the Gungans that Binks had recently traveled extensively off-world,<ref name="tsas" /> and as the battle was won by the Gungan/Naboo/Republic coalition,<ref name="tpm" /> Toba decided that he would follow in the footsteps of his new idol, and leave Naboo for the stars. The gungan took it upon himself to self-instruct about the ways of [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]], and gave himself a hazardous crash course in the skill utilizing one of Bullba's bongos.<ref name="tsas" />

===To Ohma-D'un and beyond===
In the wake of the liberation of Naboo, the Gungan people elected to colonize one of the planet's [[Moon/Legends|moons]]—[[Ohma-D'un/Legends|Ohma-D'un]]. Toba was among the first hired to help build the colony, and carved a niche for himself piloting an armed [[BullbaBong]] [[BB-2 Shuttle]], leased at a discounted rate from his father's spaceworks division. In his new role, Toba set about ferrying both cargo and workers, in addition to the sporadic tourist or [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomat]]. It soon became his goal to earn enough money from his shuttle run to purchase a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] for the ship and ultimately work outside the [[Naboo system/Legends|Naboo system]]. After months of working his shuttle run and modifying the leased vehicle, Toba was accepted into the [[Naboo Royal Space Fighter Corps/Legends|Naboo Royal Space Fighter Corps]], who were accepting Gungans as part of their new peace accords in the wake of the liberation of Naboo. The youthful Gungan was among the first to be trained by the Royal Space Fighter Corps pilots, and in time Toba became a volunteer in the service of [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Amidala]].<ref name="tsas" />

Toba found it difficult to adapt to the regimented nature of serving in the military after his time working freelance—nevertheless he persisted with his new career, determined to establish himself as a pilot of note. Along with several other Gungan pilots in the same program, Toba formed [[Iron Cesta Flight]], a unit made up of [[G-1 starfighter]]s, which were a joint venture between BullbaBong and [[Theed Palace Space Vessel Engineering Corps/Legends|Theed Palace Space Vessel Engineering Corps]] and were designed for longer periods of independent flight and combat in-system than their [[N-1 starfighter/Legends|N-1 starfighter]] predecessors. Iron Cesta Flight's duty was to patrol the Naboo system for any [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] looking to harass those commuting between Naboo and Ohma-D'un. After two years of service Toba was offered the opportunity to command Iron Cesta Flight, but the Gungan had still not fully adapted to the military life. With a windfall from a wealthy [[Toba's aunt|aunt]], and his savings, he left the RSF Corps, and decided to pursue his dream of life beyond the Naboo system.<ref name="tsas" />

The Gungan used his pooled resources to purchase a small [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the [[B'zabuu-class transport|''B'zabuu''-class transport]] ''[[Sea Killer]]'', and a used [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]], [[R5-R5]].<ref name="tsas" /> Within a month, the Gungan had made several cosmetic and armament modifications to the craft.<ref name="tsk">{{Wizards|url=starwars/article.asp?x=sw20010222d&c=rpg|text=The ''Sea Killer''}}</ref> Toba was also able to purchase several contracts from a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Toba's smuggler friend|friend]] who had incurred several debts with the [[Hutt Cartel/Legends|Hutt Cartel]]. The Gungan still called Ohma-D'un home, where he kept his base of operations. In his new career as a smuggler, Toba frequented the [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] identifying as an "independent trader." The Gungan tried to ensure that for the most part, his smuggling practices were never to the detriment of BullbaBong—in turn, Bullba turned a blind eye to his son's illegal activities.<ref name="tsas" />

==Personality and traits==
Toba was one to risk his life adventuring even in his youth, partaking in various activities that were considered hazardous to one's health. He was a brave explorer, and also had a thirst for the heat of combat. Drawing inspiration from his commanding officer in the Gungan Grand Army, Bombad General Binks, Toba developed a yearning to leave Otoh Gunga and Naboo at an early age to pursue a career offworld. Toba could also be an obstinate individual, directly disobeying his mother when he joined the Gungan Grand Army. He was also not one for tutelage, instead choosing to learn skills by his own hand. Toba's lifestyle clashed with the structured regime of military life, which adversely impacted his time in Naboo's RSF Corps. Ultimately, Toba found himself in the smuggling trade, and would attempt to make sure that his more untoward activities did not impact his father's business.<ref name="tsas" />

===Appearance, attire, and skills===
Toba had brown skin, and wore shoulder pads on his upper body. He also wore flight goggles. Toba could speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]] and [[Gungan (language)|Gungan Basic]].<ref name="tsas" />

==Equipment and vehicles==
Toba owned a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] and later a heavier model of such a weapon, a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], and a tool kit, among other items. His first ship was a BullbaBong shuttle, which he did not own outright and was in fact leased from his father's manufacturing company. In his later service with the Naboo Royal Space Fighter Corps, Toba possessed a pair of pilot's goggles, and flew a G-1 starfighter, which was also a co-development from his family's holdings. Toba was eventually able to purchase his own ship, the used ''B'zabuu''-Class Transport ''Sea Killer''.<ref name="tsas" /> After a month of ownership, the Gungan had the visage of an opee sea killer painted on the nose of the ''Sea Killer'', causing the freighter to resemble a 25-meter-long flying fish to others. Just in case the ship's engines were not adequate to escape trouble, Toba also modified the craft with a "Getaway" [[Concussion missile launcher/Legends|missile launcher]] scavenged from a [[Corellian Engineering Corporation/Legends|Corellian Engineering Corporation]] [[YG-4400 light freighter]].<ref name="tsk" />

==Appearances==
*[[Star Wars: Invasion of Theed Adventure Game|''Star Wars: Invasion of Theed'' Adventure Game]] {{1st}}
